findings.4.7(1): CONFIRMED normal (I, I² integrally closed; single Newton facet x+2y+2z≥3)
findings.4.7(2): ERRATUM: NOT integrally closed — xz² ∈ closure(I) ∖ I since (xz²)² = (x²z)(z³) ∈ I²; μ(Ī) = 8 > 7, so Theorem 3.1 (needs I = Ī, μ ≤ 7) does not apply as printed. Independent of the Main Theorem (Example 4.5 verified separately).

reference.citation: Ataka, M., & Matsuoka, N. (2026). Normality of monomial ideals in three variables (arXiv:2602.01782). arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.01782

reading: General monomial-ideal normality instrument (integral-dependence membership + RRV d=3) independently verifying a Feb-2026 paper: 4.7(1) confirmed normal; 4.7(2) is a kernel-checkable erratum (not integrally closed). Verification-amplification; no trust surface. The erratum is minor (illustrative example) and independent of the Main Theorem.
